# Private Repository & BOM Override


::: {.scenario}
*Or what to Do When You Need Enterprise Support*
This scenario shows how to incorporate a private, third-party repository that provides patched or extended-support versions of dependencies. It specifically demonstrates how a third-party Bill of Materials (BOM) can be used to override a publicly available, EOL (End-of-Life) dependency with a patched version from the private repository.
This is a common enterprise pattern when a vendor provides long-term support for a library (like Spring Boot) after its official EOL date.
:::

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    subgraph Public Repos
        SpringBoot["org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter:2.5.14 (EOL)"]
    end
    subgraph Private Repo
        PatchedStarter["org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter:2.5.14.ACME"]
        VendorBOM["com.thirdparty.springboot:patched-bom:1.0.0"]
        VendorBOM -. manages .-> PatchedStarter
    end
    Root[Project Root] -- imports --> VendorBOM
    Root --> SpringBoot_Declared("spring-boot-starter (version from BOM)")

```

#### Gradle (build.gradle)
The `build.gradle` file first adds the private repository. Then, it imports the vendor's BOM using the `platform()` function. The actual dependency is declared without a version, letting the imported BOM control which version is used.

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-16-private-bom/build.gradle" snippet="repo-and-deps"}
```

#### Reader Expectation
A private repository combined with a `platform()` (BOM) allows seamless overrides of public, EOL dependencies with patched vendor versions.

#### Why This Happens
Private repositories provide custom artifacts, and `platform()` constraints enforce their selection. This enterprise pattern ensures builds prioritize supported patches over official, unmaintained releases.

#### Key Takeaway
Adding a repository makes its artifacts *available*, but it is the combination of the repository and a version control mechanism (like a BOM imported with `platform()`) that allows you to safely override public dependencies with private, patched versions.
